title: Lemonade Has Momentum, but Investors Need More Than Growth
text: Lemonade has momentum but investors need more than growth analysis. Company showing strong operational progress with 32% in-force premium growth in 2026 and revenue growth of 71% in Q1 2026. However, profitability remains elusive despite dramatic improvements in loss ratios and EBITDA losses. Path to profitability dependent on maintaining premium growth rates while scaling operating leverage. Morgan Stanley upgrade on Tesla partnership demonstrates analyst confidence in autonomous vehicle insurance opportunity. Pet insurance segment become largest business line, surpassing $500 million in-force premium. Analyst views mixed: consensus rating of Hold, average price target $64-66. Stock trades at significant premium to traditional insurers despite yet-to-be-profitable status. Investors focusing on when company reaches GAAP profitability (guidance suggests 2027 or later). Key risks include catastrophic losses (California wildfires cost $20 million net in Q4 2024) and competitive threats from traditional insurers.
